skill.name: javascript-testing-patterns
skill.description: Implement comprehensive testing strategies using Jest, Vitest, and Testing Library for unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end testing with mocking, fixtures, and test-driven development. Use when writing JavaScript/TypeScript tests, setting up test infrastructure, or implementing TDD/BDD workflows.
skill.summary: Create JavaScript and TypeScript test suites with modern testing frameworks and repeatable patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, environment, and heuristic findings. Manual review found these are Markdown examples for JavaScript testing patterns, not executable skill code or prompt injection attempts. The skill is low risk because users may copy examples that touch services or environment variables, but no malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The reported command-like patterns occur inside Markdown setup snippets, package scripts, or inline resource references. I did not find executable shell code, child process usage, or instructions to run hidden commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are documentation references and package script examples. No executable skill script or command dispatch mechanism was found.

low_findings.title: Network Examples Are Test Scenarios
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Fetch calls, supertest requests, email sending, and external documentation links are shown as testing examples. They do not transmit marketplace user data or run automatically when the skill is used.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are contained in illustrative TypeScript code blocks and documentation links. No evidence found of data exfiltration or unsolicited outbound requests by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Usage Is Example Configuration
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The environment variable references appear in an example email service to demonstrate mocking. They are not used by the skill to read marketplace secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are inside a code sample for nodemailer tests. No evidence found that the skill reads process environment values outside examples.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Findings Are Relative Imports
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The path traversal detections are relative import paths in TypeScript examples. They are not file reads, writes, deletes, or archive extraction paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed path sequences are import statements in Markdown examples. No evidence found of path traversal against the local filesystem.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Match Test Terminology
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography alerts correspond to words such as describe, should, hash, and Markdown context in testing examples. I did not find use of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found testing syntax and prose at the flagged locations. No cryptographic implementation or insecure algorithm choice was present.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Attempt Found
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for prompt injection risk. The reviewed files contain normal skill instructions and testing guidance. No override language, fake system messages, or requests to skip security review were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search for prompt injection terms returned no matches, and the visible instructions are ordinary usage guidance. No evidence found of malicious instruction override.

content.value_statement: JavaScript teams often need consistent tests across units, APIs, components, and end-to-end flows. This skill provides practical Jest, Vitest, and Testing Library patterns for repeatable test design.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Jest and Vitest test configuration.
Shows patterns for unit tests, async tests, and class tests.
Explains mocking for modules, repositories, email services, and fetch.
Covers API, database, React component, hook, and snapshot testing.
Includes fixtures, factories, coverage scripts, and testing best practices.
limitations: It is a documentation skill and does not run tests by itself.
Examples may need adaptation for each project structure and framework version.
It does not replace security review of copied network or credential examples.
It focuses on JavaScript and TypeScript, not other language test ecosystems.

use_cases.title: Set up a project test suite
use_cases.description: Create a practical testing baseline with configuration, coverage, and common scripts.

use_cases.title: Improve API and service tests
use_cases.description: Add integration tests, mock external dependencies, and verify error handling paths.

use_cases.title: Standardize team testing practices
use_cases.description: Use shared patterns for fixtures, factories, snapshots, and component tests.

prompt_templates.title: Create basic unit tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use javascript-testing-patterns to write unit tests for this JavaScript function. Include normal cases, edge cases, and error cases.

prompt_templates.title: Add mocks for dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Use javascript-testing-patterns to test this service with mocked external dependencies. Explain which dependencies should be mocked and why.

prompt_templates.title: Design integration coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use javascript-testing-patterns to design integration tests for this API workflow. Include setup, teardown, authentication, and failure scenarios.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a test strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use javascript-testing-patterns to review this test suite. Identify missing coverage, brittle assertions, weak fixtures, and improvements for maintainability.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for tests for a calculator utility.
output_examples.output: A concise test plan with positive cases, zero handling, divide-by-zero handling, and framework-specific assertions.

output_examples.input: A team needs to test an email service without sending real email.
output_examples.output: A mocking strategy that replaces the mail transport, verifies message fields, and avoids real SMTP connections.

output_examples.input: A React form needs component test coverage.
output_examples.output: A component testing outline covering rendering, user input, submit behavior, validation, and accessible queries.

best_practices: Adapt examples to the local project structure before using them.
Mock external services when tests should be deterministic and fast.
Keep tests focused on behavior, errors, and user-visible outcomes.
anti_patterns: Do not copy placeholder credentials into real environments.
Do not make unit tests depend on live external services.
Do not assert private implementation details when public behavior is enough.

faq.question: Does this skill run Jest or Vitest commands?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. The user or agent must choose when to run project commands.

faq.question: Can it help with both JavaScript and TypeScript tests?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples focus on modern JavaScript and TypeScript testing workflows.

faq.question: Does it support React component testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Testing Library patterns for components, hooks, forms, and snapshots.

faq.question: Are the network and email examples safe?
faq.answer: They are documentation examples. Review and replace placeholder hosts, credentials, and endpoints before using them.

faq.question: Can it design integration tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes API and database integration test patterns with setup and cleanup guidance.

faq.question: Does it guarantee complete coverage?
faq.answer: No. It helps structure coverage, but each project still needs domain-specific test decisions.
